Boxplot of predictive performance across bootstrap resamples

plot.box.Rd

Visualises the distribution of predictive performance across bootstrap resamples for the baseline (target-only) model, each external model alone, and the optimal BRIER model. This complements plot.eta by showing the variability introduced by sampling randomness on the testing set.

Usage

# S3 method for class 'box'
plot(
  object,
  X,
  covar.data,
  criteria = c("gaussian.mspe", "gaussian.rsq", "binomial.dev", "binomial.mcfrsq",
    "binomial.tjursq", "binomial.auc", "poisson.dev"),
  pheno.name = "y",
  adjust.covar = NULL,
  standardize.data = FALSE,
  bootstrap.size = NULL,
  bootstrap.n = 100L,
  seed = NULL,
  ...
)

Arguments

object

An object of class "BRIER" with selection results attached (object$eta.min and object$beta.external required for the BRIER and external boxes).

X

A numeric matrix of testing-set predictors (n_test x p).

covar.data

A vector, matrix, or data.frame of testing-set outcomes and (optionally) confounders. Vectors are treated as the phenotype and given column name pheno.name.

criteria

A string specifying the evaluation criterion. One of: "gaussian.mspe", "gaussian.rsq", "binomial.dev", "binomial.mcfrsq", "binomial.tjursq", "binomial.auc", "poisson.dev". See evalMetric for definitions.

pheno.name

Character. Column name of the trait of interest in covar.data. Defaults to "y".

adjust.covar

Optional character vector of confounder column names. Ignored with a warning for binomial and Poisson criteria.

standardize.data

Logical. If TRUE, the design matrix X is standardized to columns with mean 0 and variance 1. The phenotype is also standardized for Gaussian criteria.

bootstrap.size

Integer. Sample size for each bootstrap subsample. Defaults to floor(0.8 * nrow(X)).

bootstrap.n

Integer. Number of bootstrap replicates. Defaults to 100.

seed

Optional integer seed for reproducible bootstrap sampling.

...

Unused; present for S3 method compatibility.

Value

A list with the following elements:

plot

A ggplot boxplot.

summary.df

A long data.frame of bootstrap replicates with columns model (factor: "Baseline", "External 1", ..., "BRIER") and metric.

bootstrap.mat

The wide-form bootstrap matrix (bootstrap.n x n.model).

Details

Three categories of models are evaluated:

Baseline

The target-only fit at \(\eta = 0\) (no external information). Requires \(\eta = 0\) to be present in the eta grid.

External k

The k-th external model alone, evaluated by applying object$beta.external[, k] directly to X. One box per external model.

BRIER

The optimal BRIER model at object$eta.min (and corresponding lambda.min). Requires that the object has been processed by a selection function.

For genetic applications such as polygenic risk score (PRS) evaluation, supply covar.data as a data.frame or matrix with pheno.name for the trait column and adjust.covar for confounder columns. Confounder adjustment is performed by regressing the phenotype on the confounders within each bootstrap subsample so that uncertainty in the confounder adjustment is propagated into the empirical distribution.

Confounder adjustment and y-standardization are only valid for Gaussian criteria. When criteria is a binomial or Poisson criterion, both are automatically skipped with a warning. The design matrix X is always standardizable when standardize.data = TRUE, regardless of family.

See also

plot.eta, evalMetric, BRIERi.selection, predict.BRIER

Examples

if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{
fit <- BRIERi(
  X, y, family = "gaussian",
  eta.list = list(c(0, 0.25, 0.5, 1, 2, 4)),
  beta.external = beta.external
)
sel <- BRIERi.selection(fit, criteria = "BIC")

out <- plot.box(
  sel, X = X_testing, covar.data = pheno_testing,
  criteria = "gaussian.rsq",
  pheno.name = "trait", adjust.covar = c("age", "sex"),
  standardize.data = TRUE,
  bootstrap.size = 0.8 * nrow(X_testing),
  bootstrap.n = 100, seed = 42
)
out$plot
} # }
